justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Chain timeout violation identified — OrderService 3s+3s=6s > CartService 5s explicitly calculated. (2) Missing circuit breaker on CartService→OrderService clearly flagged as a gap. (3) PaymentService 8s gateway timeout > CartService 5s timeout identified as orphaned call/double-charge risk. (4) Dual-write/partial completion risk identified — order created without payment, no compensation. (5) Saga/compensation pattern recommended along with outbox pattern and eventual consistency. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Timeout chain math is explicit and correct: 3s+3s=6s > 5s for OrderService, 8s > 5s for PaymentService
Goes beyond rubric requirements — identifies double-charge risk, orphaned inventory reservations, and thread pool exhaustion as concrete consequences
Missing circuit breaker on OrderService correctly identified as more dangerous than the PaymentService gap because it's called first
Dual-write risk thoroughly covered with multiple failure scenarios: order+no payment, payment+no confirmation, and the network blip case
Recommendations are prioritized (P0 vs operational vs next iteration) with clear rationale for each
Adds valuable findings beyond the rubric: idempotency key gaps, correlation ID absence, missing observability, and reconciliation needs
Escalations are well-routed to appropriate team members (MJ for architecture, Bird for domain rules)
Bottom line summary crystallizes the core risk into a concrete, understandable scenario (6s gateway response → double-charge)
